tool_definitions.name: TodoWrite
tool_definitions.description: Write the agent's to-do list. Pass the complete replacement list — all current items will be replaced. Items are auto-cleared when all reach 'completed' status. Use status 'in_progress' for the task currently being worked on (max 1 at a time).

tool_definitions.name: EnterPlanMode
tool_definitions.description: Switch to plan mode for complex tasks that require exploration and design before any file changes. In plan mode you MUST NOT write or edit files — only read, search, and explore. When ready, call ExitPlanMode to present your plan for approval.

tool_definitions.name: ExitPlanMode
tool_definitions.description: Present your completed plan and request approval to exit plan mode and begin implementation. Call this only after you have written a thorough plan.

tool_definitions.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_definitions.description: Present structured questions to the user and wait for their answers before continuing. Use when you need to clarify approach, preferences, or missing information. Each question must offer 2+ predefined choices. Limit to 4 questions per call.

tool_definitions.name: Glob
tool_definitions.description: Fast file pattern matching. Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time (most recent first).

tool_definitions.name: Grep
tool_definitions.description: Content search powered by ripgrep (with native fallback). Supports regex, file type filtering, multiline matching, context lines, and paginated output.

tool_definitions.name: ToolSearch
tool_definitions.description: Search available tools by keyword or fetch exact tools by name. Use "select:Name1,Name2" for exact lookup, or keywords for fuzzy search.

tool_definitions.name: agent_spawn
tool_definitions.description: Spawn a named child agent to complete a specific task. Returns the child agent's final output as a string. Call task_delegate first to mark the task as delegated on the board, then pass its task_id here so the child's result can be matched back.

steps.message: MCP transport: connecting
MCP transport: connected

You are a coordinator helping refactor a codebase. Follow these steps exactly:
1. Call AskUserQuestion with ONE question: 'Which refactoring scope?' with options 'Targeted — one module only' and 'Full — all modules'.
2. Call EnterPlanMode.
3. Call TodoWrite with THREE tasks: id='explore' content='Explore repo' status='in_progress', id='write-plan' content='Write plan' status='pending', id='dispatch' content='Spawn refactor worker' status='pending'.
4. Call Glob with pattern='*.json' to explore.
5. Call TodoWrite: 'explore' completed, 'write-plan' in_progress, 'dispatch' pending.
6. Call TodoWrite: 'write-plan' completed, 'dispatch' in_progress.
7. Call ExitPlanMode.
8. Call agent_spawn with agentName='refactor-worker' and description='Refactor the targeted module based on the approved plan'.
9. Call TodoWrite: 'dispatch' completed (auto-clear fires — all done).
Report what the user chose, the worker's output, and confirm the list was cleared.

results.content:
I'll follow the steps exactly as specified. Let's begin!

**Step 1: Ask the user about refactoring scope.**

options.label: Targeted — one module only
options.description: Refactor only a single, specific module in the codebase.

options.label: Full — all modules
options.description: Refactor all modules across the entire codebase.

results.content:
{"message":"Entered plan mode. Explore the codebase and design your approach.

In plan mode you should:
1. Read and search files to understand existing patterns
2. Identify similar features and architectural approaches
3. Consider trade-offs between multiple approaches
4. Use AskUserQuestion if you need to clarify the approach
5. When ready, call ExitPlanMode to present your plan

DO NOT write or edit any files yet."}

tools.name: TodoWrite
tools.description: Write the agent's to-do list. Pass the complete replacement list — all current items will be replaced. Items are auto-cleared when all reach 'completed' status. Use status 'in_progress' for the task currently being worked on (max 1 at a time).

tools.name: EnterPlanMode
tools.description: Switch to plan mode for complex tasks that require exploration and design before any file changes. In plan mode you MUST NOT write or edit files — only read, search, and explore. When ready, call ExitPlanMode to present your plan for approval.

tools.name: ExitPlanMode
tools.description: Present your completed plan and request approval to exit plan mode and begin implementation. Call this only after you have written a thorough plan.

tools.name: AskUserQuestion
tools.description: Present structured questions to the user and wait for their answers before continuing. Use when you need to clarify approach, preferences, or missing information. Each question must offer 2+ predefined choices. Limit to 4 questions per call.

tools.name: Glob
tools.description: Fast file pattern matching. Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time (most recent first).

tools.description: Content search powered by ripgrep (with native fallback). Supports regex, file type filtering, multiline matching, context lines, and paginated output.

tools.description: Search available tools by keyword or fetch exact tools by name. Use "select:Name1,Name2" for exact lookup, or keywords for fuzzy search.

tools.description: Spawn a named child agent to complete a specific task. Returns the child agent's final output as a string. Call task_delegate first to mark the task as delegated on the board, then pass its task_id here so the child's result can be matched back.

results.content:
Here's the full summary of what happened:

---

### ✅ Refactoring Workflow Complete

#### 1. 🎯 User Choice
The user selected **"Targeted — one module only"** — meaning the refactor is scoped to a single specific module, not the entire codebase.

---

#### 2. 🤖 Worker Output
The `refactor-worker` agent was spawned and returned:
> **"Task delegated to refactor-worker: Refactor the targeted module based on the approved plan — result: done"**

The worker completed its task successfully.

---

#### 3. 🗂️ To-Do List Status
All three tasks progressed through their lifecycle and were marked **completed**:

| Task | Status |
|---|---|
| `explore` — Explore repo | ✅ Completed |
| `write-plan` — Write plan | ✅ Completed |
| `dispatch` — Spawn refactor worker | ✅ Completed |

Since every task reached `completed` status simultaneously, the **auto-clear fired** — the list was wiped clean (`"cleared": true`, `"todos": []`). ✨ The board is now empty.
